LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC. DBA GREAT WESTERN COLLECTION BUREAU'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE PROHIBITING RICHARD B. COLE FROM SELLING ANY LIQUOR LICENSES AS A COURT APPOINTED RECEIVER. ARGUED AND THE COURT GRANTED THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. THE COURT PROHIBITED MR. COLE FROM ACTING AS RECEIVER ON ALL SAN FRANCISCO CASES AND BARRED HIM FROM SELLING ANY LIQUOR LICENSES FOR 90 DAYS IN ANY OTHER COUNTY COURT CASES TO GIVE OPPOSING COUNSEL TIME TO SEEK REMOVAL WITHIN 45 DAYS. COURT FURTHER ORDERED BOTH SIDES TO NOTIFY OTHER COURTS THAT MR. COLE IS BARRED FROM SELLING LIQUOR LICENSES IN THEIR CASES IN OTHER COUNTIES. MR. COLE TO FILE RESIGNATIONS IN ALL THOSE CASES IN SAN FRANCISCO NO LATER THAN 06/24/11 AND IS BARRED FROM DOING ANYTHING FURTHER ON THOSE CASES. NEW RECEIVERS TO BE APPOINTED IN THOSE CASES THAT ARE CURRENTLY IN ESCROW. PARTIES TO MEET & CONFER TO PROVIDE LIST OF THOSE CASES CURRENTLY IN ESCROW. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

Plaintiff'S Order To Show Cause To Defendant Richard Cole
Off Calendar Jun-17-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC. DBA GREAT WESTERN COLLECTION BUREAU'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE PROHIBITING RICHARD B. COLE FROM SELLING ANY LIQUOR LICENSES AS A COURT APPOINTED RECEIVER.

APPEARANCES: Douglas B. Provencher, Esq. of Provencher & Flatt LLP 707/284-2380 for pltf & moving party; Richard B. Cole, 408/297-2587 receiver & opposing party.

RULING: Argued and the Court granted the preliminary injunction. The Court prohibited Mr. Cole from acting as receiver on all San Francisco cases and barred him from selling any liquor licenses for 90 days in any other county court cases to give opposing counsel time to seek removal within 45 days. Court further ordered both sides to notify other courts that Mr. Cole is barred from selling liquor licenses in their cases in other counties. Mr. Cole to file resignations in all those cases in San Francisco no later than 06/24/11 and is barred from doing anything further on those cases. New receivers to be appointed in those cases that are currently in escrow. Parties to meet & confer to provide list of those cases currently in escrow. Plaintiff to submit a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
